title: Dot notation doesn't work in Array(JSON) columns
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
When a column of type `Array(JSON)`, trying to get a value of the first element of the first JSON of each array with dot notation fails. It's needed to cast to String and then use JSONExtract functions.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
25.4.1.2934
### How to reproduce
```sql
CREATE TABLE test (
id UInt64,
locations Array(JSON)
) ENGINE = MergeTree ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO test (id, locations) VALUES
(1, []),
(2, [
'{"id": 1607532, "name": "Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya", "latitude": 14.0, "admin1_id": 1607530, "admin2_id": null, "continent": "AS", "longitude": 101.0, "country_id": 1605651, "admin1_code": "14", "admin1_name": "Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya", "admin2_code": "1401", "admin2_name": null, "admin3_code": null, "admin4_code": null, "continent_id": 6255147, "country_code": "TH", "feature_code": "PPLA", "feature_class": "P"}'
]),
(3, [
'{"id": 1607532, "name": "Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya", "latitude": 14.0, "admin1_id": 1607530, "admin2_id": null, "continent": "AS", "longitude": 101.0, "country_id": 1605651, "admin1_code": "14", "admin1_name": "Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya", "admin2_code": "1401", "admin2_name": null, "admin3_code": null, "admin4_code": null, "continent_id": 6255147, "country_code": "TH", "feature_code": "PPLA", "feature_class": "P"}',
'{"id": 1605651, "name": "Thailand", "latitude": 15.0, "admin1_id": null, "admin2_id": null, "continent": "AS", "longitude": 100.0, "country_id": 1605651, "admin1_code": null, "admin1_name": null, "admin2_code": null, "admin2_name": null, "admin3_code": null, "admin4_code": null, "continent_id": 6255147, "country_code": "TH", "feature_code": "PCLI", "feature_class": "A"}'
]),
(4, [
'{"id": 1605651, "name": "Thailand", "latitude": 15.0, "admin1_id": null, "admin2_id": null, "continent": "AS", "longitude": 100.0, "country_id": 1605651, "admin1_code": null, "admin1_name": null, "admin2_code": null, "admin2_name": null, "admin3_code": null, "admin4_code": null, "continent_id": 6255147, "country_code": "TH", "feature_code": "PCLI", "feature_class": "A"}'
]);
SELECT
id,
locations[1].id
FROM test
Query id: 0d856f09-038e-4990-b6be-c12225731837
Elapsed: 0.034 sec.
Received exception from server (version 25.4.1):
Code: 43. DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000. DB::Exception: First argument for function tupleElement must be tuple or array of tuple. Actual JSON: In scope SELECT id, tupleElement(locations[1], 'id') FROM test. (ILLEGAL_TYPE_OF_ARGUMENT)
```
Using JSONExtract functions on JSON also fails because they expect a String type, not JSON
```sql
SELECT id, JSONExtractInt(locations[1], 'id') FROM test;
SELECT
id,
JSONExtractInt(locations[1], 'id')
FROM test
Query id: 0da75bfb-bc25-48cd-b573-e76a2804072d
Elapsed: 0.026 sec.
Received exception from server (version 25.4.1):
Code: 43. DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000. DB::Exception: The first argument of function JSONExtractInt should be a string containing JSON, illegal type: JSON. (ILLEGAL_TYPE_OF_ARGUMENT)
```
And the only thing I found to work was casting to string, which I imagine is probably less performant than just querying the native JSON type:
```sql
SELECT id, JSONExtractUInt(locations[1]::String, 'id') FROM test; -- works
SELECT
id,
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test2 (id BIGINT, location ARRAY<STRING>);
INSERT INTO test2 (id, location) VALUES (1, ARRAY[]), (2, ARRAY['{"id": 1, "name": "Location A"}']), (3, ARRAY['{"id": 2, "name": "Location B"}', '{"id": 3, "name": "Location C"}']);
SELECT id, JSON_EXTRACT(location[1], '$.name') AS name FROM test2;
SELECT id, JSON_EXTRACT(location[1], '$.id') AS id FROM test2;
SELECT id, CAST(JSON_EXTRACT(location[1], '$.id') AS INTEGER) AS id FROM test2;
key_bug_features: Dot notation fails for Array(JSON) column access
